definition: Present, appearing or found everywhere, especially to the point of being very common.

sentences.text: Industrial robots have become _____ in modern automotive production.
Wireless communication is now _____ across most manufacturing facilities.
Data collection systems are increasingly _____ in smart factories.

definition: Completely clear and leaving no possibility for doubt or misunderstanding.

sentences.text: The test results provided _____ evidence that the sensor was defective.
The customer gave an _____ instruction to stop production immediately.
The safety procedure must be written in _____ language.

definition: Belonging naturally or essentially to something rather than being caused by external factors.

sentences.text: Precision is an _____ requirement of high-quality robotic welding.
The design has an _____ limitation that cannot be solved through software alone.
Traceability is _____ to any reliable quality-management system.

definition: Apparently or according to what is stated, although the reality may be different.

sentences.text: The modification was _____ introduced to improve safety, but it also reduced cycle time.
The machine was _____ ready for production, although several alarms remained active.
The meeting was _____ about scheduling, but most of the discussion focused on cost.

definition: To find a way around a problem, restriction or requirement, often through an indirect method.

sentences.text: The team created a temporary software solution to _____ the communication limitation.
Safety functions must never be disabled to _____ production delays.
We redesigned the fixture to _____ the robot's restricted working envelope.

definition: To make an existing problem, difficult situation or negative condition worse.

sentences.text: Increasing the welding current may _____ the spatter problem.
Poor cable routing can _____ intermittent communication faults.
Delaying maintenance will only _____ the mechanical wear.

definition: Extremely careful and precise, with close attention to every detail.

sentences.text: The commissioning engineer kept _____ records of every parameter change.
A _____ inspection revealed a barely visible crack in the fixture.
Robot calibration requires a _____ approach to achieve repeatable results.

definition: A model, pattern or established way of thinking about how something should be done.

sentences.text: Digital manufacturing has introduced a new _____ for production management.
Collaborative robots are changing the traditional automation _____.
The company shifted from reactive maintenance to a predictive maintenance _____.

definition: The complex and often unintended consequences of a decision, action or event.

sentences.text: The team considered the safety _____ of modifying the robot sequence.
A production delay of this scale could have serious financial _____.
The manager explained the long-term _____ of selecting an unsupported control system.

definition: Able to recover quickly from difficulties, failures or changing conditions.

sentences.text: A _____ production system can recover quickly after a device failure.
The network architecture must be _____ enough to tolerate temporary communication loss.
The company invested in a more _____ supply chain after repeated shortages.
